title: fumana ericoides subsp. montana
intro: Fumana ericoides subsp. montana, also known as mountain rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a shrub-like perennial with pink to purple flowers, native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of Fumana ericoides subsp. montana include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
